attacking_style: Initiates attacks late in races (inside 10km) on climbs, often serving as catalyst rather than finisher. Prefers using local knowledge and terrain familiarity to time efforts on home climbs.
team_tactics: AG2R uses him as primary option over teammates when betting on late disruption. Attacks solo to prevent sprints when team lacks confidence in leadout positioning.
terrain_preference: Home climbs and familiar terrain where local knowledge provides advantage. Capable on Monument-level climbs but best as aggressor rather than finisher. Struggles on flat roads after climbing efforts.
vulnerability: Cannot sustain efforts after early breakaway participation—fades in finale after extended work. Lacks sprint finish to convert late attacks into wins when caught.
key_patterns: Attacks late inside 10km mark, documented in Tour de Suisse and Romandie finales.
Functions as catalyst for winning moves rather than winner—initiated Nibali's Lombardia attack sequence.
Fades after early breaks, insufficient depth for sustained multi-hour efforts shown twice.
AG2R prioritizes him over teammates for late-race disruption tactics when sprint uncertain.
Strong late-race surges but lacks finishing speed to convert, nearly caught leaders in Romandie.
form_trajectory: positive: 2019 Swiss races showed improved late-race strength on home terrain with successful climbs and near-catches, upgrading from 2018 fade issues
